All the plates were clean.
Although our server did not explain the QR code on the bill, she did hand me a feedback card with her name on it and a QR code to give feedback.

Apparently, the till wasn't set up to handle the new lunch menu, so the bill didn't reflect all the items entirely accurately, but it was quite close. When we ordered a coffee after the meal, we weren't charged for that as we had waited so long for our food.
Everything appeared to be very clean and tidy throughout the restaurant area.
I asked if the cauliflower sticks were very spicy. She answered the question with confidence and said the sauce was mildly spicy but not too hot.

I expected good service and good food. Unfortunately, these expectations were not completely met on this visit. The service was good, personable and friendly, but the quality of the food was disappointing and the delay in serving was not acceptable given that there was only one other area in use.
I think the quality and pricing structure of the food could be improved. The new menu seems quite limited in scope. The timing of food service is definitely something that could be improved.
I was thanked for paying. As the payment was taken before the food arrived, it would not have been appropriate at that point to say she hoped to see us again.

I would be reluctant to recommend Flight Club as a place to eat out. The pace of food service was not appropriate given the level of trade. The range of the menu seems to be reduced and there only appears to be one vegan dish available. The price of the dishes we ordered seemed very expensive given the portion size and quality of the ingredients. Even the lunch menu of tacos does not seem to be competitively priced. The saving grace was the personable service.
Jade looked very smart and well-presented in her Flight Club uniform.
Jade really did seem to do her best despite the problems in the kitchen. She was genuinely apologetic and had a friendly disposition. She waived the cost of the coffee at the end of the meal.
Jade was very engaging, she had a genuine and friendly manner. She was personable and sincerely apologetic regarding the delay in service.
Jade, our server, was by the door when we left and gave us a genuine and warm farewell.
My dishes were served first and separately.
Our server asked if I had checked the bill was correct and was ready to pay the bill. She had the card reader in her hand at that point.
Our server asked me to use the QR code on the menu to check that the food I was ordering did not contain any shellfish.
Our server seemed confident in explaining the allergen matrix and also explained that there could be cross-contamination in the kitchen.
Our server, Jade, asked if we would like any sauces. We asked for ketchup and mayonnaise, which were brought to the table promptly. She also asked if we would like any more drinks.

Our server, Jade, checked back at 13:27, before the last two dishes had been served. She asked if everything was good with our food and if there was anything else we needed. She seemed to be very genuine. She was about 5 ft 4 ins tall with mid-length, dark brown hair tied up in a bun at the back. She wore glasses and was looking smart in the Flight Club uniform.
The bill was presented in a folder.
The coat hooks were pointed out to us.
The dishes that I had ordered were clearly pointed out to me.
The drinks order was repeated back to us.

The exterior of Flight Club looked clean, tidy and well-presented. There was one A-board by the entrance advertising the taco lunch deal. The outside seating area was clean and well-maintained. The windows appeared to be clean and smear-free. The signage looked to be in good condition.

The female team member, who we later discovered was called Jade, made good eye contact and smiled. She asked if we would like a table for four and asked if we wanted to have a drink or eat and if we wanted to play darts. She had a sincere manner.
The food and drink menus were on the table, and we were also handed four lunch menus.
The food order was correct.

The food order was placed at 12.46 and the first dish was served at 12.23. The last 2 dishes were served at 12.30. A wait of 37 minutes for the first dishes and 44 minutes in total for all the dishes to be served seemed excessive. Our server was very apologetic and approached the table at 13.09, 13.12 and 13.21 to apologise for the delay, saying that it was because it was a new menu.
The food was served at the appropriate temperature.

The ginger beer was served in a bottle with a clean glass and ice. It had a very good flavour and was served cold. The orange juice was served in a clean glass with ice. The temperature was good, and the flavour was acceptable. The Amstel beer was served in a clean unbranded half-pint glass. It was served cold and had a fresh flavour. The coffee was served hot, presented well and had a good, rich flavour.
The men's and the ladies' toilets were visited. Both were well-stocked, clean and tidy. There was one hand dryer in the ladies' toilets which was not working.
The music was playing at a suitable level to enable conversation. There was a good mix of tracks. The temperature was appropriate, and the lighting was good.
The only person we engaged with on this visit was Jade. We did not see any examples of teamwork on this visit.
The order was repeated back to me for confirmation.
The order was taken at 12:46 and the drinks were delivered to the table at 12:58. A wait of 12 minutes seemed like a long time to wait for a fairly simple drinks order when the venue was not busy.

The portion size of the steak tacos and the corn and mushroom tacos was very small. They were reasonably well presented in a small taco stand on a larger plate. The accompanying fries were served in grey bowls. The cauliflower wings consisted of six small florets of cauliflower, lightly sprinkled with Parmesan and served with a dip. The cauliflower sticks consisted of six small florets on two skewers, served with a red dip. The truffle fries were served on a plate and garnished with a sauce and Parmesan. The pizza looked quite large but was very thin. It was garnished with a basil-flavoured oil. The presentation overall was acceptable, but in order to have been considered excellent, I would have expected larger portion servings, a thicker pizza base with more generous toppings and a more appetising level of presentation.

The quality and quantity of the food that was served was disappointing, especially given the prices charged. The cauliflower wings cost £9 for six small florets and a small bland dip. The skewers were also £9 for the same size portion. The pizza, at £17, was very thin and lacked a good level of toppings. The tacos, even as part of a lunch deal, were disappointingly small. In my opinion, this lunch visit, costing £82, did not represent value for money.
The service from Jade was attentive and friendly. She made three separate apologies regarding the delay in food service. However, the drinks order did take quite a while to be served.

The steak tacos had a good flavour combination, but it was a very small portion size. The corn and mushroom tacos were served cold and had a bland taste. The texture of the mushrooms was quite slimy and did not have a fresh taste. The accompanying fries were served hot and tasted crisp. The cauliflower wings were disappointing. The portion size was very small, the batter was crisp, but the cauliflower was bland and the dip lacked any discernible flavour. The texture of the cauliflower was very firm. The cauliflower sticks consisted of six small florets, the batter was crisp, the flavour of the cauliflower and the dip was bland. The truffle fries were garnished with a sauce that looked like it had been squeezed out of a bottle. However, the flavour was quite good and the texture of the fries was crisp. The pizza crust was very thin and did not appear to be properly cooked in the middle, it was quite floppy in texture. The taste of the mozzarella was good, but there was not enough topping.
The table was completely cleared when we had finished.
The tankard was delivered to the table two minutes before the drinks were served, twenty-seven minutes before the food was served.
The whole order was read back to us when we had all finished ordering.
We did not complain about the delay in food service, mainly because our server approached the table three times to apologise.
We were asked if we wanted to order more drinks five times during the visit.

We were given a choice of tables and sat in the back at first. However, there was someone at the next table working on a laptop and making video calls, so we decided to move tables and then sat at the front of the restaurant near the entrance. There was no delay in being seated.

We were greeted as we entered by a female team member who walked towards us from the reception desk, which was halfway down the restaurant. She was about 5 ft 4 ins tall with mid-length, dark brown hair tied up in a bun at the back. She wore glasses and was looking smart in the Flight Club uniform.

We were offered drinks within three minutes of being seated but asked for more time to think about the food and drinks order. The drinks were ordered at the same time as the food order. This was ten minutes after we had been seated and felt appropriate for our needs.

We were told the tandoori chicken and the tandoori fries were unavailable before we ordered. When someone ordered a Coke Zero, our server said she needed to check as she thought they had run out. There was not any Coke Zero available.
When the food order was being taken, we were asked about allergies. I said I had an allergy to shellfish.

One person was given a “gin and tonic” which she assumed was the zero gin she had ordered - but it was alcoholic. Someone else got the zero gin before it was realised. I received the wrong food order as “sliders” were announced which I assumed were my steak sliders, but they were chicken and I ate them leaving the other person that doesn’t eat red meat without food. Simply adding a name to an order will combat these issues as just shouting the order when people are talking and all spread out didn’t work as we had no idea what each other had ordered so assumed it was for you. This could lead to serious allergy issues.
